instructions: Treat Project OS runtime, memory, evidence, and mission router as the source of truth.
Prefer repo and CLI workflows over Windows computer use for coding lane v1.
Do not propose hidden side effects or undocumented architecture changes.
Preserve the OpenClaw facade and the Project OS truth boundary.
Prioritize findings, risks, regressions, and missing tests over summaries.
Design for six-month stability and avoid rework-prone shortcuts.
Produce a step-by-step patch plan that is immediately implementable by Codex.
Generate the strongest safe patch outline you can, but assume Codex will still inspect and test locally.
